name: American Near East Refugee Aid (Anera)
mission:
Since 1968, Anera has helped refugees and others hurt by conflicts in the Middle East live with dignity and purpose.
Anera, which has no political or religious affiliation, works on the ground with partners in Palestine (West Bank and Gaza), Lebanon and Jordan. We mobilize resources for immediate emergency relief and for sustainable, long-term health, education, and economic development. Our staff serve in their communities, navigating the politics that constrict progress to get help where it's needed most. That's how Anera delivered more than $100.4 million in programs in 2020 alone, and it's how we will keep building better lives until hope finds its way in the Middle East.
theoryOfChange: For Women Can, Anera states that supporting women to generate their own income is a reliable strategy for enhancing resilience and building a future for their families, implemented through training, seed funding, mentorship, equipment procurement/installation, and ongoing coaching/mentoring to help enterprises develop, sustain, and scale.

scoreSummary: American Near East Refugee Aid (Anera) shows exceptional alignment and good impact, with zakat compliance (significant risk deductions applied).

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: 77% of reported revenue is noncash (gifts-in-kind)
keyConcerns.detail: Noncash contributions inflate reported program ratios. Cash-adjusted metrics better reflect actual spending.

keyConcerns.headline: 65% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations
keyConcerns.detail: Organization claims foreign operations but most spending remains domestic.

components.evidence: Explicit zakat program (+4); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Asnaf: fuqara (+5); Operates in Muslim-majority regions (+3); Strong humanitarian service (+4)

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.

components.improvement_suggestion: Build reserves toward your board-approved target range (often 3-12 months), adjusted for revenue volatility and obligations.

score_details.score_summary: American Near East Refugee Aid (Anera) shows exceptional alignment and good impact, with zakat compliance (significant risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.anera.org/zakat (Source: https://www.anera.org/zakat)

all_citations.claim: Anera holds a 100.0/100 score, a 96.3% program expense ratio, and a fundraising efficiency of $0.01 per $1 raised.
Anera is Zakat-eligible and maintains a specific zakat policy for donors.
The organization reported total revenue of $204,317,485 and working capital of 1.2 months.
The organization has a long history of distributing in-kind grants and cash awards for humanitarian relief.
Anera offers multiple donation options and giving methods for supporters.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: Anera earns a strong overall rating due to its exceptional financial efficiency and long-standing track record of delivering direct services in high-urgency humanitarian zones. Its perfect transparency score and Zakat eligibility make it a highly aligned choice for Muslim donors.
areas_for_improvement: Relatively low working capital of 1.2 months, which may limit long-term financial flexibility
High cost per beneficiary relative to some smaller-scale direct service interventions

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization shows high alignment for donors seeking to address urgent humanitarian needs in the Middle East, with a long history of service dating back to 1968 [1]. Anera is Zakat-eligible and provides clear policies for donors to fulfill their religious obligations through specific asnaf categories [2][9]. Its consistent focus on refugee dignity and purpose aligns with high-priority humanitarian causes [1].
dimension_explanations.impact: Anera demonstrates high impact through direct service delivery, managing a total revenue of $204,317,485 to facilitate large-scale distributions of in-kind medical supplies and cash grants [1][3][7]. While the cost per beneficiary is calculated at approximately $14,656.88, the organization maintains a 96.3% program expense ratio, indicating that nearly all resources are directed toward its core missions of health, education, and economic development [1][3].

baseline_narrative.headline: American Near East Refugee Aid (Anera) provides critical humanitarian relief and sustainable development to refugees and vulnerable communities across the Middle East.
strengths: Exceptional program expense ratio of 96.3%, ensuring the vast majority of funds reach those in need
Highly efficient fundraising operations with a cost of only $0.01 per $1 raised
Zakat-eligible programs that adhere to Islamic giving principles for humanitarian relief
baseline_narrative.summary: founded in 1968, Anera delivers large-scale humanitarian aid, including medical supplies and educational support, to those affected by conflict in the Middle East [1][3]. The organization maintains a perfect Charity Navigator score of 100.0/100, reflecting its commitment to transparency and financial health [1].

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: Anera earns a strong overall rating due to its exceptional financial efficiency and long-standing track record of delivering direct services in high-urgency humanitarian zones. Its perfect transparency score and Zakat eligibility make it a highly aligned choice for Muslim donors.

areas_for_improvement.context: The organization currently maintains <cite id="13">1.2 months of working capital</cite>, which is on the lower end for an institution of this size, potentially limiting flexibility during sudden revenue volatility.
Approximately <cite id="14">77% of revenue is derived from non-cash contributions</cite> (GIK), which can create valuation complexities and sensitivity to supply chain shifts.

bbb_assessment.summary: Anera maintains high governance standards with a large, independent board and audited financials.

case_against.mitigation_notes: Anera mitigates these risks through its 50+ year track record and deep institutional partnerships that ensure a steady flow of medical supplies.
risk_factors: <cite id="14">77% of revenue is non-cash</cite>, which can fluctuate based on corporate donor availability.
<cite id="13">Working capital of 1.2 months</cite> is lean for an organization operating in high-risk conflict zones.
case_against.summary: The primary considerations for donors include the <cite id="14">high proportion of Gift-in-Kind (GIK) revenue</cite> and a relatively high cost per beneficiary.

confidence.notes: Extremely high data availability and transparency from the organization.

known_gaps: High GIK ratio requires careful interpretation of program efficiency

alignment.explanation: The charity is a strong fit for Muslim donors, offering <cite id="16">verified zakat-eligible programs</cite> that serve the poor (fuqara) and needy (masakin) in <cite id="17">Muslim-majority regions like Gaza and Lebanon</cite>.
alignment.improvement: Alignment could be further strengthened by <cite id="18">expanding services to underserved geographies</cite> with even more limited nonprofit coverage.

credibility.explanation: Donors can place high trust in Anera due to its <cite id="10">perfect scores in Accountability, Culture, and Leadership</cite> from Charity Navigator and its <cite id="9">Platinum Seal from Candid</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To further enhance credibility, the organization could seek to reduce its <cite id="14">77% GIK inflation risk</cite> by diversifying cash revenue streams.

impact.explanation: Anera demonstrates high impact through <cite id="3">96.3% program expense</cite> and a <cite id="15">clear theory of change</cite> focused on both relief and dignity for refugees.
impact.improvement: Impact scores could improve by <cite id="13">building reserves toward a higher target range</cite> to ensure long-term sustainability beyond immediate crisis cycles.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade B - Outcome tracking with clear Theory of Change

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Distributes in-kind medical supplies and cash grants to local clinics and schools.

rich_narrative.headline: American Near East Refugee Aid (Anera) provides critical humanitarian relief and sustainable development to refugees and vulnerable communities across the Middle East.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support established, large-scale humanitarian operations in the Middle East with a focus on high financial efficiency and zakat compliance.
donor_motivations: Want to fulfill zakat obligations through a reputable, long-standing institution
Prioritize organizations with extremely low fundraising overhead
Care about the long-term dignity and health of Palestinian and Lebanese refugees
giving_considerations: Gifts support a large-scale operation where 77% of aid is in-kind medical supplies
The organization operates in highly volatile political environments
Focus is on direct service delivery rather than advocacy
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking small-scale, grassroots projects or those who prefer organizations with high cash-to-GIK ratios.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Anera tracks <cite id="20">3 specific outcome metrics</cite> and has a long history of audited impact, though it lacks RCT-level evidence.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: Anera utilizes a <cite id="15">documented theory of change</cite> that moves from emergency relief to sustainable community development in health, education, and agriculture.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that large-scale medical distributions actually reach intended beneficiaries and improve health outcomes.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: The ongoing humanitarian crises in Gaza and Lebanon create an urgent and expanding need for Anera's logistics and aid capacity.
strategic_priorities: Sustainable agriculture in the West Bank

peer_comparison.differentiator: Anera stands out with a <cite id="3">100.0% program ratio</cite> compared to the <cite id="19">peer median of 90.3%</cite>.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Global reach vs. Anera's Middle East specialization
similar_organizations.name: Action Against Hunger USA

similar_organizations.differentiator: Intergovernmental agency vs. Anera's NGO status
similar_organizations.name: UNRWA

strengths.detail: Anera directs <cite id="3">96.3% of its expenses toward programs</cite>, supported by a highly lean fundraising model that costs just <cite id="4">$0.01 per $1 raised</cite>.
Operating since <cite id="7">1968</cite>, the organization has built a massive network that distributed <cite id="8">$63,867,775 in foreign grants</cite> to 438 recipients in 2023.
The charity has earned the <cite id="9">Candid Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite> and maintains <cite id="10">perfect scores across all Charity Navigator beacons</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: Anera's operational model is characterized by its ability to manage large-scale logistics in conflict zones. In 2023, the organization managed <cite id="1">over $173 million in revenue</cite>, with a significant portion consisting of in-kind medical supplies and humanitarian goods. Their <cite id="11">3-year revenue CAGR of 24.1%</cite> demonstrates robust growth and an increasing capacity to absorb funding for Middle East crises. Furthermore, the organization maintains <cite id="12">96.3% independent board members</cite>, ensuring rigorous oversight of its diverse programs in agriculture, health, and emergency response.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 1968, American Near East Refugee Aid (Anera) has established itself as a leading humanitarian organization dedicated to serving refugees and vulnerable communities in the Middle East. The organization operates at a significant scale, reporting <cite id="1">total revenue of $173,279,297</cite> in the 2023 fiscal year. Anera's mission focuses on providing <cite id="2">emergency relief and sustainable development</cite> across Palestine, Lebanon, and Jordan. The charity is distinguished by its exceptional financial efficiency, maintaining a <cite id="3">program expense ratio of 96.3%</cite>, which ensures that donor contributions are fully utilized for direct service delivery. This efficiency is further highlighted by a <cite id="4">fundraising efficiency of $0.01 per $1 raised</cite>, one of the lowest in the humanitarian sector. Beyond immediate crisis response, Anera invests in <cite id="5">long-term infrastructure, education, and health programs</cite>, leveraging over five decades of regional expertise to navigate complex geopolitical landscapes. The organization holds a <cite id="6">perfect 100/100 rating from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting top-tier performance in accountability, culture, and leadership.
